title: Cercestis kamerunianus
intro: Cercestis kamerunianus, also known as Kamerunian climbing arum, is a flowering plant species in the family Arumaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in West African folk medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, likely due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cercestis kamerunianus include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it may also be used to make a paste or ointment for topical application.
taxonomy: Cercestis kamerunianus is a species of flowering plant in the family Staphylocactaceae. It is a type of succulent plant that is native to the tropical regions of Central and West Africa. This plant belongs to the genus Cercestis, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their succulent leaves and stems. The classification of Cercestis kamerunianus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Asparagales. As a member of the Staphylocactaceae family, Cercestis kamerunianus is closely related to other succulent plants in the same family.

The plant's extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may help in treating various infections.
